articles.title: ESCAPING THE CHROME SANDBOX THROUGH DEVTOOLS
articles.summary: Vulnerabilities in Chromium allowed malicious Chrome extensions to execute arbitrary shell commands. By exploiting undocumented features in the chrome://policy page and a race condition in the chrome.devtools API, users were able to bypass the sandbox and execute code outside the browser's restrictions.

articles.title: WHY DOES EVERYONE RUN ANCIENT POSTGRES VERSIONS?
articles.summary: While Postgres 17 offers huge improvements, most users stick with older versions due to the perceived stability of the database and the challenges of upgrading. Major version upgrades can be complex and require extensive planning, data migration, and testing, especially for large production databases. However, delaying upgrades inevitably leads to technical debt. There is now a tool available called PG Versions that can help visualize the new benefits of upgrading earlier rather than later.

articles.title: EVAL-DRIVEN DEVELOPMENT: BUILD BETTER AI FASTER
articles.summary: Evals, a new testing paradigm for AI-native development, are similar to end-to-end tests. They assess the quality of AI output using automated checks, human judgment, and AI-assisted grading. Vercel's frontend-generating AI product v0 is built on eval-driven development.

articles.title: EVALUATION QUIRKS, METRIC PITFALLS, AND SOME RECOMMENDATIONS
articles.summary: There are a few issues with system evaluation practices, like in cases where metrics with similar names yield vastly different results, implementation bugs lead to inflated scores, and incorrect predictions improve the score. Developers need to be careful when choosing metrics and understand what a metric truly measures to make sure it's implemented correctly.

articles.title: UNIT TESTS AS DOCUMENTATION
articles.summary: Unit tests can serve as living documentation within the code itself, explaining how code behaves, staying in sync with code changes, and covering edge cases.

articles.title: INTERESTING STARTUP IDEA: BENCHMARKING CLOUD PLATFORM PRICING
articles.summary: Spare Cores is a startup that benchmarks cloud platform pricing, offering a free service that compares prices across providers like AWS, GCP, Azure, and Hetzner. The company was started with a €150K grant and a team of three. It uses a combination of Python, Angular, and various databases to collect and analyze data.

articles.title: TOLGEE
articles.summary: Tolgee is an open-source localization platform. It provides features like in-context translation, translation memory, and integration with machine translation services.

articles.title: SAVA OS
articles.summary: Sava OS is a desktop interface for web browsers that allows users to manage, filter, share, and organize their online data in a familiar environment. It has features like tab management, session control, personal feed creation, and folder sharing.

articles.title: NOTEBOOKLM UPDATE: AUDIO OVERVIEW CONTROLS AND TEAM COLLABORATIONS
articles.summary: Google's NotebookLM now allows users to customize audio overviews. There is a pilot program for a business-focused version with enhanced features.

articles.title: NOT REMOTELY COOL: THE SCIENCE OF “ZOOM FATIGUE”
articles.summary: Zoom fatigue is caused by the cognitive load of online meetings, which depletes mental resources. Video calls require more effort to process nonverbal cues and the audio gaps caused by digital transmission disrupt the natural flow of conversation. The lack of physical proximity and the blurring of work and home life also make things mentally tough.

articles.title: USING CLOUDFLARE ON YOUR WEBSITE COULD BE BLOCKING RSS USERS
articles.summary: Cloudflare's security features, specifically Bot Fight Mode and AI Scrapers and Crawlers, are unintentionally blocking RSS readers from accessing websites. This happens because Cloudflare's AI-based system incorrectly identifies RSS readers as malicious bots, presenting them with human verification challenges they can't complete.

articles.title: BRAIN DRAIN: DAVID VS GOLIATH
articles.summary: The demand for data to train large AI models is exceeding the availability of public data. Researchers are exploring synthetic data as an alternative, but it can introduce errors and degrade model performance. A new approach focuses on data quality over quantity, demonstrating that smaller, highly-trained models can compete with larger ones.

articles.title: THE AI OPPORTUNITY: UPDATES TO OUR GEMINI AND KNOWLEDGE & INFORMATION TEAMS
articles.summary: Google is restructuring its Knowledge & Information team to optimize for the Gemini era, moving the Gemini app team to Google DeepMind and the Assistant teams to the Platforms & Devices org.

articles.title: APPLE PASSWORDS' GENERATED STRONG PASSWORD FORMAT
articles.summary: Apple Passwords' generated strong password format uses gibberish two-syllable "words" by design to make them easier to remember and type on various keyboards while maintaining strong entropy.

articles.title: OPENVMM
articles.summary: OpenVMM is a modular, cross-platform, general-purpose Virtual Machine Monitor (VMM) written in Rust by Microsoft.

articles.title: ADOBE'S NEW IMAGE ROTATION TOOL IS ONE OF THE MOST IMPRESSIVE AI CONCEPTS WE'VE SEEN
articles.summary: Adobe's new AI-powered "Project Turntable" allows users to rotate 2D vector images in 3D while maintaining their original 2D appearance and shape.
